untranslatable_reason: Signal requires three fundamental inputs not available in the allowed indicator set: (1) IPO date detection and day-counting (meta-data, not a price/technical indicator), (2) phonetic simplicity scoring of ticker symbols (linguistic/NLP analysis), and (3) company name fluency metrics (linguistic/NLP analysis). These are qualitative/structural features requiring external data sources and computation outside standard OHLCV technical indicators. Backtest engine would need custom data pipeline for IPO calendars and NLP-based linguistic scoring.
translation_notes: The Kahneman cognitive ease signal is theoretically sound but operationally impossible to implement in a pure technical indicator framework. Entry conditions depend on: event metadata (IPO timing), ticker/company name linguistic analysis (not available), and fluency percentile rankings (requires external NLP library). Exit conditions (day 3 close or +2.5% gain) are translatable but meaningless without valid entry logic. Recommend: build custom pre-filter for IPO screening + integrate phonetic scoring library (e.g., Metaphone, Soundex) + add linguistic complexity metrics before passing to backtest engine.
